title: Renovate’s update PRs filed too frequently for digest dependencies
body:
https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/1904 was filed on 2023-02-11; merged on 2023-02-23.
Just three hours later, https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/1924 was filed.

https://github.com/containers/automation/blob/fada0fa488981a2b1ddd9d3f1debf71571143cba/renovate/defaults.json5#L112-L117 suggests that a new PR should be filed on the first day of the month (only), and IIRC that used to work.

@cevich do you know what is happening? (This is low-priority, the PRs are easy to ignore.)

comments.body: I don't recall changing this config but I'll look into it.  Thanks for the references.
Curiously both mentioned PRs say in their description: "Schedule: Branch creation - At any time".  So definitely the mentioned rule is not being used for some reason.

After merging https://github.com/containers/automation/pull/130 I ticked the "rebase" box on https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/1924  In a moment, renovate should re-run on that PR and (hopefully) update the schedule.

The update happened and changed the description to read:

"date Schedule: Branch creation - "after 1am and before 11am on the first day of the month" in timezone UTC, Automerge - At any time (no schedule defined)."

So my guess at a fix seems to have worked :tada:
